source.title: Structural Damage - Walkway Detachment at Devils Tower Platform

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 773

actions_taken: Two Nabors employees opened Room 77 C door and moved to safety
Third employee moved to safe distance on walkway
Stairway and walkway secured with chain bindings
Affected area cordoned off with red danger tape
Third floor personnel relocated to alternative accommodations
All tabs correctly welded as of 09 September 2025
NDT inspections scheduled for completion by 15 October 2025
event.summary: On 16 July 2025 at 1810 hours, an external walkway platform on the third floor of a Temporary Living Quarters (TLQ) at Devils Tower SPAR platform unexpectedly detached from the TLQ structure by 12-18 inches while three Nabors employees were standing on it. The walkway remained held by stanchions and stairway connections. Investigation revealed inadequate welded tabs that lacked sufficient surface area for long-term support, combined with 23 years of marine corrosion and fatigue. The TLQ was originally designed as temporary housing in 2002 but remained in service. Visual-only inspections failed to detect weld deterioration. No injuries or dropped objects occurred due to immediate securing actions.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends Office of Incident Investigations consider issuing a Safety Alert
Eni to ensure future design and construction of temporary structures account for potential long-term use
Eni to incorporate dedicated inspections and protocols for TLQs focusing on marine corrosion, fatigue and weld conditions
Eni created specific annual inspection requirements for TLQ landings and tab welds in CMMS
key_phrases: deteriorated condition of the welded tabs

hazards.name: Structural failure energy from inadequate support

threats.name: Inadequate weld design for long-term use
Temporary structure used beyond design life

consequences.name: Personnel injury from fall or structural collapse

controls.name: Welded tab connections for walkway support
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier preventing structural failure

supporting_text: the welded tabs securing the walkway to the TLQ structure were not sufficient to provide a long-term permanent attachment point
The tabs lacked welded surface area which would have provided additional support
These welded tabs suffered fatigue and material degradation due to progressive corrosion

controls.name: Cross bracing for walkway structural support
controls.lod_basis: Secondary structural support to prevent failure

supporting_text: a lack of cross bracing on the south side of the walkway could have contributed to additional stress and structural vulnerability of the failed section

controls.name: Visual inspection of TLQ structural components (May 2024)
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring and verification to detect degradation before failure

supporting_text: a visual inspection was performed on the TLQ. This visual inspection was deemed by Eni to be inadequate to detect welding defects
Because Eni only performed a visual inspection of the TLQ's structural components, the inspection was unable to identify the deteriorated condition of the welded tabs

The most recent Level 1 survey that was conducted in May 2024 did not indicate any discrepancies in the walkways or structures for the TLQ. This was most likely due to only visual inspections having been performed

controls.name: Asset Integrity Management Strategy Level 1 surveys
controls.lod_basis: Management system to ensure ongoing structural integrity

supporting_text: Eni has implemented an Asset Integrity Management Strategy that required Level 1 surveys of all Eni platforms' structural components
inspection criteria did not identify or consider the possible environmental effects and age-related degradation of the welds

controls.name: Design criteria for temporary structures
controls.lod_basis: Design phase control to ensure adequate structural capacity

supporting_text: the TLQ was originally installed on the platform in 2002 and was only intended as temporary accommodations
The TLQ was then left on Devils Tower for twenty-three years
the method in which the tabs were welded was meant to simplify any future demolition or relocation of the TLQ

The TLQ was designed and constructed to be used as temporary housing for overflow workers. The TLQ remained on site for twenty-three years after original installation, which it was not originally intended for

controls.name: Stanchions bolted to TLQ
controls.lod_basis: Backup physical restraint preventing complete detachment

supporting_text: The walkway platform was still held in place by stanchions that were bolted to the TLQ

controls.name: Stairway bolted to 2nd level TLQ
controls.lod_basis: Backup physical restraint preventing complete detachment

supporting_text: the stairway beneath the walkway was bolted to the 2nd level of the TLQ

controls.name: Immediate securing with chain bindings
controls.lod_basis: Immediate action to prevent escalation after initial failure

supporting_text: The stairway and walkway were immediately secured to prevent any dropped objects and/or further separation of the walkway platform from the TLQ
Due to the immediate action taken to secure the walkway, there were no injuries or dropped objects associated with the incident
Immediately after all individuals were in safe locations, the stairway and walkway were secured with chain bindings

controls.name: Area cordoning and danger tape installation

supporting_text: the affected area was cordoned off, and red danger tape was installed around the area to warn personnel of the hazards in the area
All personnel that were housed on the third floor living quarters were then relocated to alternative accommodations, using an alternative walkway

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
